metric_value: $1.226 billion Teradata division revenue
notes: NCR Teradata division revenue at time of incident

metric_value: Approximately 700 Teradata systems installed worldwide; ~100 new systems per year
notes: Teradata market penetration; dominant in large-scale DW

metric_value: 40-node Teradata system; grown from original 28 nodes (1997) to 34 (2000) to 40 (2002)
notes: Progressive expansion reflecting growing data warehouse investment

metric_value: $16.7 million total Teradata hardware investment ($10.2M original + $6.5M expansions)
notes: Total cumulative hardware spend on Teradata platform

metric_value: 25+ applications in 3 classes: Class 1 (static/ad-hoc); Class 2 (daily batch); Class 3 (ODS real-time)
notes: Safeway had built extensive application ecosystem on Teradata

metric_value: FM-200 fire suppression system discharged on February 22 2002 at Consonus Portland data center
notes: The triggering incident for the entire damages claim

metric_value: 144+ hours of complete system downtime following the fire suppression discharge
notes: Total period of zero availability for Safeway's Teradata system

metric_value: 111 hours of degraded performance after initial recovery
notes: System partially restored but running at reduced capacity

metric_value: 359.5 person-hours of IT staff time for incident recovery
notes: Direct labor cost of restoring systems and applications

metric_value: Multiple applications required partial or complete rewrites after data corruption from the incident
notes: Beyond hardware: software and data integrity were compromised

metric_value: Planned application rollouts delayed by the incident — opportunity cost beyond direct damages
notes: Strategic IT roadmap disrupted by the incident

metric_value: Risk of permanent loss of data confidence — users may not trust data warehouse outputs after corruption event
notes: Intangible but critical risk to DW value proposition

metric_value: Risk of undetected data loss — some corrupted data may not be identified until much later
notes: Long-tail risk of data integrity issues

metric_value: Business interruption during recovery — grocery retail operations depend on daily/weekly data cycles
notes: Retail business critically dependent on timely data

metric_value: Risk of losing NCR maintenance coverage — incident may have voided warranty or maintenance terms
notes: Potential loss of vendor support for the $16.7M system

metric_value: Inability to evolve the system during recovery period — platform frozen while competitors advanced
notes: Competitive risk during extended recovery

metric_value: $1,311,542.76 in direct recovery costs (labor; hardware repair/replacement; emergency services)
notes: Itemized direct costs of incident recovery

metric_value: $11,742,608.18 in direct replacement costs (new hardware; new software; migration; installation)
notes: Cost to replace damaged/compromised Teradata infrastructure

metric_value: $2,939,903.00 in business interruption damages (lost productivity; delayed projects; opportunity cost)
notes: Quantified business impact of the incident

metric_value: $15,994,053.94 total damages claimed (direct recovery + direct replacement + business interruption)
notes: Sum of all three damage categories

metric_value: Safeway migrated from Informix/Sun to Teradata due to Informix performance limitations and Sun hardware constraints
notes: Strategic decision to invest in purpose-built DW vs general-purpose RDBMS

metric_value: Informix acquired by IBM in 2001 for $1B — validating Safeway's decision to migrate away
notes: Informix ceased as independent database vendor

metric_value: Sun Microsystems acquired by Oracle in 2010 for $7.4B — SPARC platform eventually discontinued
notes: Both components of Safeway's prior DW platform were acquired

metric_value: Teradata spun off from NCR as independent company in 2007; became publicly traded on NYSE
notes: Teradata gained independence; validated as viable standalone business

metric_value: Safeway acquired by Albertsons/Cerberus Capital Management in 2015 for $9.4B
notes: Safeway ceased as independent company; merged into Albertsons

metric_value: Safeway's Teradata implementation was among the more sophisticated in grocery retail — 25+ applications across 3 tiers
notes: Extensive DW deployment for a grocery retailer in 2002

metric_value: Walmart operated the world's largest Teradata system — Safeway benchmarked its DW ambitions against Walmart and Kroger
notes: Industry context: Walmart was the DW gold standard in retail

metric_value: Used Teradata equipment had limited resale market — replacement cost significantly exceeds residual value
notes: Factor in damages calculation: equipment depreciation vs replacement cost

metric_value: Safeway was building an Operational Data Store (Class 3 applications) for real-time retail data — most advanced tier of DW deployment
notes: ODS represented Safeway's next-generation data strategy

metric_value: Trickle feed was a custom Safeway application for real-time data loading into the ODS layer
notes: Proprietary application; represented significant development investment

metric_value: $199,688 in damages attributed to Call Center application recovery and rebuild
notes: Appendix A worksheet: Call Center application

metric_value: $1,680,000 in damages attributed to CRM S&T application — largest single application damage claim
notes: Appendix A worksheet: CRM application was most costly to recover

metric_value: $773,391 in damages attributed to Fuel application recovery
notes: Appendix A worksheet: Fuel program application

metric_value: $279,949 in damages attributed to Groceryworks (online grocery) application
notes: Appendix A worksheet: early e-grocery platform

metric_value: Data warehouse is a strategic asset — loss or degradation would permanently impair Safeway's competitive position in grocery retail
notes: Expert opinion on strategic importance of DW investment

metric_value: Teradata platform expected to remain the leading purpose-built data warehouse for large-scale retail analytics
notes: Assessment of Teradata's market position

metric_value: Teradata remains active as independent company in 2025; shifted to cloud (Teradata Vantage) but faces competition from Snowflake and Databricks
notes: Teradata survived but market position challenged by cloud-native competitors

metric_value: Damages calculated using three-tier methodology: direct recovery costs; direct replacement costs; business interruption costs — standard approach for IT disaster claims
notes: Aberdeen's damages framework

metric_value: Expert opinion: full replacement preferred over repair due to data integrity concerns and risk of latent hardware damage from fire suppression chemicals
notes: Rationale for the $11.7M replacement cost claim

metric_value: Safeway's DW investment trajectory — from $10.2M initial to $16.7M total — demonstrated increasing strategic commitment to analytics-driven retail
notes: Progressive investment pattern showed growing DW reliance
